name: async-profiler (Low-Overhead JVM Profiler)

auth.notes: Attaches to JVM process; may need root or same user

performance.notes: <1% overhead using AsyncGetCallTrace; safe for production

agent_readiness.notes: Low-overhead JVM profiler that solves safepoint bias. asprof -d 30 -f output.html PID. Output formats: flamegraph (HTML), JFR, collapsed. Modes: cpu, alloc (allocation), wall, lock, nativemem. Attaches to running JVM. IntelliJ IDEA integration. JVM agent: -agentpath:/path/to/libasyncProfiler.so=start,event=cpu,file=output.jfr. Avoids safepoint bias of JVMTI profilers. Used by Netflix, Datadog. Best JVM CPU flamegraph tool.

gotchas: Frame pointer restoration needed for accurate stacks in some JVMs
JVM agent attach syntax differs between platforms
Safepoint events still needed for allocation profiling
best_when: Production JVM CPU profiling (Spring Boot, Kafka, Spark)
Memory allocation profiling to find GC pressure
Flamegraph generation for Java performance bottlenecks
Lock contention analysis in multithreaded Java applications
avoid_when: Non-JVM languages (use py-spy for Python, bpftrace for native)
Simple development profiling (IntelliJ IDEA profiler easier)
summary: async-profiler is the gold-standard JVM profiler. Avoids safepoint bias for accurate CPU profiling. asprof -d 30 -f output.html PID generates interactive flamegraph. Modes: cpu, alloc, wall, lock. <1% overhead — safe for production. JVM agent mode for embedded profiling. Used by Netflix and Datadog for Java performance analysis. Best tool for Java/Kotlin/Scala production profiling.
